content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with 2N2N2N nodes. Each node iii has an associated value AiA_iAi‚Äã.

It is guaranteed that each integer from 111 to NNN occurs exactly **twice** in the array AAA.

Define a function F(B)F(B)F(B) for an array BBB of length MMM as follows:


Start with a score of 000.
Repeat until BBB is empty:

Choose any integer EEE present in BBB.
Let LLL be the leftmost index of EEE in BBB, and RRR be the rightmost index.
Add R‚àíLR - LR‚àíL to the score.
Remove all occurrences of EEE from BBB.
Note that after removal, the indices of remaining elements may change.


F(B)F(B)F(B) is the **minimum** possible final score achievable through any sequence of these operations.
For any ordered pair of nodes (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) in the tree, let C(u,v)C(u,v)C(u,v) denote the sequence of values of nodes on the unique path from uuu to vvv (both inclusive).

Your task is to calculate:

‚àëu=12N‚àëv=u2NF(C(u,v))\displaystyle{\sum_{u=1}^{2N} \sum_{v=u}^{2N} F(C(u,v))}u=1‚àë2N‚Äãv=u‚àë2N‚ÄãF(C(u,v))

As this sum may be very large, compute it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains a positive integer NNN, where the number of nodes in the tree is 2N2N2N.
The second line contains 2N2N2N space-separated integers, A1,A2,‚Ä¶,A2NA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{2N}A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,A2N‚Äã ‚àí-‚àí denoting the array AAA.
The following 2N‚àí12N-12N‚àí1 lines contain two space-separated integers uuu and vvv, denoting an edge between nodes uuu and vvv. It is guaranteed that the given edges form a tree.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the required sum modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.
It is guaranteed that each element from 111 to NNN occurs exactly **twice** in AAA.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The tree consists of nodes 111 and 222, each having a value 111.
The required sum is F(C(1,1))+F(C(1,2))+F(C(2,2))F(C(1, 1)) + F(C(1, 2)) + F(C(2, 2))F(C(1,1))+F(C(1,2))+F(C(2,2)):

C(1,1)C(1, 1)C(1,1): Path from node 111 to node 111 consists only of node 111 and thus C(1,1)=[A1]=[1]C(1, 1) = [A_1] = [1]C(1,1)=[A1‚Äã]=[1]. Here, F([1])=0F([1]) = 0F([1])=0 since there is only one occurrence of 111.
C(1,2)C(1, 2)C(1,2): Path from node 111 to node 222 consists of nodes 111 and 222 and thus C(1,2)=[A1,A2]=[1,1]C(1, 2) = [A_1, A_2] = [1, 1]C(1,2)=[A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã]=[1,1]. Here, F([1,1])=1F([1, 1]) = 1F([1,1])=1.
C(2,2)C(2, 2)C(2,2): Path from node 222 to node 222 consists only of node 222 and thus C(2,2)=[A2]=[1]C(2, 2) = [A_2] = [1]C(2,2)=[A2‚Äã]=[1]. Here, F([1])=0F([1]) = 0F([1])=0 since there is only one occurrence of 111.

Thus, the final sum is 0+1+0=10+1+0=10+1+0=1.
Test case 222: The edges represent a line tree consisting of 444 nodes with values [1,2,2,1][1, 2, 2, 1][1,2,2,1].
The required sum is F(C(1,1))+F(C(1,2))+F(C(1,3))+F(C(1,4))+F(C(2,2))+F(C(2,3))+F(C(2,4))+F(C(3,3))+F(C(3,4))+F(C(4,4))F(C(1, 1)) + F(C(1, 2)) + F(C(1, 3)) + F(C(1, 4)) + F(C(2, 2)) + F(C(2, 3)) + F(C(2, 4)) + F(C(3, 3)) + F(C(3, 4)) + F(C(4, 4))F(C(1,1))+F(C(1,2))+F(C(1,3))+F(C(1,4))+F(C(2,2))+F(C(2,3))+F(C(2,4))+F(C(3,3))+F(C(3,4))+F(C(4,4)):

F(C(1,1))=F(C(2,2))=F(C(3,3))=F(C(4,4))=0F(C(1,1)) = F(C(2,2)) = F(C(3,3)) = F(C(4,4)) = 0F(C(1,1))=F(C(2,2))=F(C(3,3))=F(C(4,4))=0: This is because C(u,u)=[Au]C(u,u) = [A_u]C(u,u)=[Au‚Äã], and F(B)=0F(B) = 0F(B)=0 if BBB consists of only one element.
F(C(1,2))=F([1,2])=0F(C(1,2)) = F([1,2]) = 0F(C(1,2))=F([1,2])=0: The path from node 111 to 222 contains no repeated values, so no score is added. Similarly, F(C(3,4))=0F(C(3,4))=0F(C(3,4))=0.
F(C(1,3))=F([1,2,2])=1F(C(1,3)) = F([1,2,2]) = 1F(C(1,3))=F([1,2,2])=1: The path from node 111 to 333 contains two 222s. The score is thus (3‚àí2)=1(3 - 2) = 1(3‚àí2)=1. Similarly, F(C(2,4))=1F(C(2,4))=1F(C(2,4))=1.
F(C(1,4))=F([1,2,2,1])=2F(C(1,4)) = F([1,2,2,1]) = 2F(C(1,4))=F([1,2,2,1])=2: To minimise the score:

First, remove 222s: score becomes 111; and array becomes [1,1][1, 1][1,1]
Remove 111s: score becomes 222.


F(C(2,3))=F([2,2])=1F(C(2,3)) = F([2,2]) = 1F(C(2,3))=F([2,2])=1: The path from 222 to 333 contains two 222s. The score is (2‚àí1)=1(2 - 1) = 1(2‚àí1)=1.

The final sum is the total of all these values: (0+0+0+0)+(0+0+1+1+2+1)=5(0 + 0 + 0 + 0) + (0 + 0 + 1 + 1 + 2 + 1) = 5(0+0+0+0)+(0+0+1+1+2+1)=5.
Thus, the answer for this test case is 555.
